    <item>
      <title>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander ready to shoulder another champion run after incredible Game 3 win over Suns</title>
      <description>Gilgeous-Alexander had arguably the best games of his already-prolific playoff career in Saturday's 121-109 win.</description>
      <pubDate>28 Apr 2026 02:02:09 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/shai_gilgeous_alexander_ready_to_shoulder_another_champion_run_after_incredible_game_3_win_over_suns/s1_13132_43772723</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/shai_gilgeous_alexander_ready_to_shoulder_another_champion_run_after_incredible_game_3_win_over_suns/s1_13132_43772723</guid>
      <author>Eric Smithling, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/8/d/8d2ac567b2afe5e400dc9c39a896dfa4486c8a34/thumb_16x9/shai-gilgeous-alexander-ready-shoulder-another.jpg">
        <media:credit> Rick Scuteri-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander drives on Phoenix Suns guard Collin Gillespie in the first half during Game 3 of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Mortgage Matchup Center. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Oklahoma City Thunder</category>
      <category>Phoenix Suns</category>
      <category>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Karl Anthony-Towns gets Knicks back in series vs. Hawks</title>
      <description>After losing a pair of tough one-point games to put themselves in a 2-1 series deficit, the New York Knicks got back to their game on Saturday night in a thoroughly convincing 114-98 win over the Atlanta Hawks.</description>
      <pubDate>25 Apr 2026 21:11:42 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/karl_anthony_towns_gets_knicks_back_in_series_vs_hawks/s1_13132_43773542</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/karl_anthony_towns_gets_knicks_back_in_series_vs_hawks/s1_13132_43773542</guid>
      <author>Adam Gretz, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/c/e/cefa4fef937fb8c6f52bcef3361c61ff4daa837b/thumb_16x9/karl-anthony-towns-gets-knicks-back-series-vs.jpg">
        <media:credit> Dale Zanine-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> New York Knicks center Karl-Anthony Towns (32) tries to reach the basket against Atlanta Hawks forward Onyeka Okongwu (17) during the first half at State Farm Arena. </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>New York Knicks</category>
      <category>Atlanta Hawks</category>
      <category>Karl-Anthony Towns</category>
      <category>OG Anunoby</category>
      <category>Jalen Brunson</category>
    </item>
    <item>
      <title>Injuries to Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo will test Timberwolves' depth as they aim to make third straight WCF trip</title>
      <description>The depth of the Timberwolves has shone throughout their first-round playoff series against the Denver Nuggets. They'll now have to rely on it even more.</description>
      <pubDate>28 Apr 2026 02:02:09 -0400</pubDate>

    <item>
      <title>Oklahoma City Thunder sweep their way to second round</title>
      <description>Facing elimination, the Phoenix Suns didn't lie down against the Oklahoma City Thunder. It wasn't enough to avoid a four-game sweep. </description>
      <pubDate>28 Apr 2026 00:09:52 -0400</pubDate>

        <media:title> Oklahoma City Thunder guard Shai Gilgeous-Alexander (2) against Phoenix Suns forward Dillon Brooks (3) in the second half during game four of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Mortgage Matchup Center. </media:title>
      </media:content>

      <title>Rockets' Kevin Durant might already be on the trade block</title>

    <item>
      <title>Desmond Bane proving to be missing piece for the Magic</title>
      <description>As the Orlando Magic are on the verge of knocking off the Eastern Conference's top seed in the first round, they're benefiting from exactly what they were missing in the playoffs one year ago.  Desmond Bane, 27, who the Magic acquired in a summer trade with the Memphis Grizzlies, has proven to be the difference-maker against the Detroit Pistons. </description>
      <pubDate>27 Apr 2026 23:58:19 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/desmond_bane_proving_to_be_missing_piece_for_the_magic/s1_13132_43780688</link>
      <guid>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/desmond_bane_proving_to_be_missing_piece_for_the_magic/s1_13132_43780688</guid>
      <author>Mike J. Asti, Yardbarker</author>
      <media:content url="http://www.yardbarker.com/media/f/a/fa08a7f23c49e1fa8835b501523f581ea2ef489d/thumb_16x9/desmond-bane-proving-missing-piece-magic.jpg">
        <media:credit> Mike Watters-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Orlando Magic guard Desmond Bane (3) shoots a three-point basket against Detroit Pistons forward Duncan Robinson (55) during the second half of Game 4 of the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs at Kia Center.  </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Orlando Magic</category>
      <category>Detroit Pistons</category>
      <category>Memphis Grizzlies</category>
      <category>Desmond Bane</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Heat's big changes won't include Pat Riley or Bam Adebayo</title>
      <description>The Miami Heat know they need to make changes after winning just a single playoff game in the last three seasons. Monday, team president Pat Riley confirmed those changes wouldn't involve Heat center Bam Adebayo — or himself.</description>
      <pubDate>28 Apr 2026 00:48:20 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/heats_big_changes_wont_include_pat_riley_or_bam_adebayo/s1_13132_43780579</link>

    <item>
      <title>Desmond Bane stars in front of his former teammates in Game 4 win</title>
      <description>Desmond Bane is no longer a member of the Memphis Grizzlies. But playing in front of former teammates Ja Morant and Jaren Jackson, Jr., Bane was the star in a "grit-n-grind" style game, complete with bleeding arms.</description>

    <item>
      <title>Pistons inch closer to stunning first-round exit with Game 3 road loss to Magic</title>
      <description>The Detroit Pistons are halfway to a stunning first-round exit. </description>
      <pubDate>26 Apr 2026 00:06:32 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/pistons_inch_closer_to_stunning_first_round_exit_with_game_3_road_loss_to_magic/s1_13132_43772053</link>

    <item>
      <title>Timberwolves take 3-1 series lead vs. Nuggets, but suffer major injuries</title>
      <description>The Minnesota Timberwolves took a commanding 3-1 series lead over the Denver Nuggets on Saturday night with a 112-96 win.</description>
